name: Chilmark
overview: Chilmark is a pretty, historic village of around 150 houses in the Nadder Valley, about 11 miles west of Salisbury, straddling the B3089 within the Cranborne Chase National Landscape. It is famous for Chilmark Stone, a buttery limestone quarried locally from medieval times until the 1930s and used to build Salisbury Cathedral; many of the village's cottages and houses are built from it. A stream runs through the village to join the River Nadder, and the parish includes the hamlets of Mooray, Portash and Ridge.
character: A quintessential, peaceful Wiltshire stone village in the Nadder Valley, with a thriving community supporting its pub, school and church.
amenities: The Black Dog (village pub, licensed since the 15th century)
Church of St Margaret of Antioch (13th century, Grade II*)
Tisbury (3 miles) for shops, station, surgeries and leisure centre

schools.name: Chilmark and Fonthill Bishop CofE Primary School

prices.summary: A sought-after Nadder Valley village; characterful Chilmark-stone cottages and houses with strong heritage appeal command good values. See Rightmove for current sold-price detail.

thingsToDo: Church of St Margaret of Antioch and its listed chest tombs
Walks in the Nadder Valley and Cranborne Chase
Exploring the historic Chilmark stone quarries and Fonthill estate
placesToEat: The Compasses Inn at nearby Chicksgrove
pros: Beautiful, historic Chilmark-stone village in Cranborne Chase
Retains pub, primary school, church and cricket club
Tisbury's amenities and mainline station just 3 miles away
cons: No railway station in the village
Quiet and rural; limited public transport
whoItSuits: Buyers wanting a characterful Nadder Valley stone village with a pub and school, close to Tisbury's amenities and rail.
